Please describe what this PR does and why.

## ✅ Pull Request Review Checklist

## Type of Change
- [ ] Feature  
- [ ] Bug fix  
- [ ] Refactor  
- [ ] Documentation  

---

### 1. Make it work
- [ ] Linked to a user story or issue  
- [ ] PR description explains what changed and why  
- [ ] Works as expected and meets acceptance criteria  
- [ ] No obvious bugs introduced  
- [ ] No unrelated changes included  

---

### 2. Make it right
- [ ] Code is readable and well-structured  
- [ ] Naming is clear and meaningful  
- [ ] No duplicated code  
- [ ] No dead or commented-out code  
- [ ] Functions are small and focused  
- [ ] Tests added or updated where appropriate  
- [ ] Existing tests pass  

---

### 3. Make it fast
- [ ] No obvious performance issues  
- [ ] Avoids unnecessary loops or API calls  
- [ ] Efficient for current scale  

---

### 4. CI/CD checks
- [ ] CI pipeline runs on this PR  
- [ ] All checks pass (build, tests)  
- [ ] No skipped or disabled checks  
- [ ] Pipeline time is reasonable  

---

### 5. Project build
- [ ] Project builds successfully  
- [ ] No known vulnerable packages introduced  

---

### 6. Security
- [ ] No secrets or credentials in code  
- [ ] Input validation present  
- [ ] Auth and permissions handled correctly  
- [ ] Sensitive data properly protected  

---

### 7. Deployment readiness
- [ ] Feature flags used if needed  
- [ ] Database migrations are safe and reversible  

---

### 8. Documentation
- [ ] README or docs updated  
- [ ] API changes documented  
- [ ] Comments added for complex logic  

---

### 9. Git hygiene
- [ ] Commits are clean and logical  
- [ ] Branch is up to date with main  
- [ ] PR size is reasonable  

---

### Reviewer checklist
- [ ] Pull and run code locally  
- [ ] Test critical flows manually  
- [ ] Leave clear, actionable feedback  
- [ ] Approve only if production-ready
